articles.title: Nvidia shares fall nearly 5% and risk trades roll over
articles.snippet: Nvidia put its best foot forward with a big earnings beat but it isn't enough for the market. Shares were up around 1% in the pre-market but opened lower and are now down almost 5%. The drop is spreading beyond the company and the Nasdaq is down 1.7% and it's now even hitting FX with AUD/USD quickly falling 20 pips to the lows of the day.Shares of NVDA were trying to break above the current range but have been pulled back in and are now likely to test the lower end of the range.Other chipmakers

articles.title: Canada Q4 current account deficit $0.71B vs $7.71B expected
articles.snippet: Prior was -$9.68 (revised to -$5.27B)14th consecutive quarter of current account deficitsTrade in goods and services balance posted a $3.1 billion deficit in the fourth quarter vs $4.4 billion in Q3Goods deficit $4.5B vs $9.5B priorForeign investors acquired a record $33.6 billion of federal government bondsCanadian investors acquired $18.5 billion of foreign securities, down from a $56.1 billion investment in the third quarterFor the year 2025, the current account balance posted a $30.4 billion

articles.title: USD/CHF Price Forecast: Rebounds on firm USD, targets 0.7800
articles.snippet: USD/CHF recovers some ground on Thursday, rising some 0.19% as the Greenback appreciates and bounces off daily lows beneath 0.7700 on solid US data. At the time of writing, the pair trades at 0.7743, yet it remains shy of the key resistance level seen at 0.7817, the February 2 daily peak.

articles.title: Fed’s Goolsbee: I want us to be careful
articles.snippet: Austan Goolsbee, President of the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago, said interest rates can come down, but they don't want to front-load before inflation eases. He also added he wants the Fed to be careful in a Fox News Interview on Thursday.

articles.title: Silver Price Forecast: XAG/USD slides amid a firm US Dollar
articles.snippet: Silver (XAG/USD) price loses its brightness on Thursday, moving in the opposite direction of Gold, which remains steady as the US Dollar recovers some ground and rises. Stronger-than-expected US jobs data keeps the white metal pressured in the mid-North American session.

articles.title: Gold holds above $5,170 as Iran tensions offset a firm USD
articles.snippet: Gold price stays firm on Thursday during the North American session as geopolitical tensions remain elevated despite the beginning of the third round of talks between the US and Iran in Geneva. Also, solid data from the US kept bullion prices contained.

articles.title: AUD/USD declines on USD strength, hawkish RBA outlook sustains
articles.snippet: AUD/USD trades with a negative bias on Thursday as broad-based US Dollar (USD) strength weighs on the Australian Dollar (AUD). At the time of writing, the pair is trading around 0.7078, down 0.60%.

articles.title: USD/JPY retreats after two-day rise amid US labor resilience, BoJ rate hike hints
articles.snippet: USD/JPY trades around 156.20 on Thursday at the time of writing, down 0.15% on the day, slightly correcting after two consecutive days of gains.

articles.title: USD/JPY: Watching 152–159.50 range break – Scotiabank
articles.snippet: Scotiabank strategists Shaun Osborne and Eric Theoret note modest Japanese Yen gains versus the Dollar, noting outperformance within the G10 but only a partial recovery of recent BoJ‑related losses.

articles.title: GBP/USD slips as firm US jobless claims offset BoE cut bets
articles.snippet: The Pound Sterling (GBP) retreats over 0.11% on Thursday as the Greenback remains steady following a report that the number of Americans applying for unemployment benefits was below estimates last week, an indication of the resilience of the labor market.

articles.title: Gold steadies as USD gains, geopolitical risks cap downside
articles.snippet: Gold (XAU/USD) gives up earlier intraday gains on Thursday, coming under modest pressure as the US Dollar gains momentum. The metal remains confined within this week’s trading range, with markets cautious ahead of key geopolitical developments.

articles.title: USD/CAD rises toward 1.3700 as US Dollar trims earlier weakness
articles.snippet: The Canadian Dollar (CAD) pares earlier gains against the US Dollar (USD) on Thursday as the Greenback shrugs off its intraday weakness. At the time of writing, USD/CAD trades at 1.3704, rebounding from the daily low around 1.3659.

articles.title: US: Midterms, Fed leadership and USD risk – TD Securities
articles.snippet: TD Securities argues US political dynamics will shape Dollar and EMFX performance into 2026.

articles.title: Fed’s Miran: The Fed should cut a percentage point this year
articles.snippet: Federal Reserve (Fed) Governor Stephen Miran said that he has not seen anything worrisome yet in private credit despite some bumps, and added that prices right now seem stable, in an interview with Fox Business on Thursday.

articles.title: AUD/USD: Positive AUD theme despite fading risk – BNY
articles.snippet: BNY’s Head of Markets Macro Strategy Bob Savage flags AUD/USD as another Dollar-positive rebalancing candidate. While AUD has seen mostly net buying since late January, inflows have been light and positioning less stretched than in other carry or hard-asset currencies.

articles.title: USD/CHF rebounds after consecutive losses amid US labor data, safe-haven flows
articles.snippet: USD/CHF trades around 0.7740 on Thursday at the time of writing, up 0.15% on the day, snapping a series of consecutive losses. The pair is supported by a modest rebound in the US Dollar (USD), although the broader backdrop remains characterized by strong demand for safe-haven assets.

articles.title: Fed: Steady policy with later cuts – TD Securities
articles.snippet: TD Securities’ Oscar Munoz and Eli Nir argue that a stable US labor market and contained inflation should keep the Federal Reserve on hold in the near term, even as growth remains firm.

articles.title: No Fed, no safety net: Why private credit’s first real recession will be its moment of truth
articles.snippet: Private credit is easy to enter but hard to exit. Retail investors seeking fast cash could trigger a financial crisis.

articles.title: These increasingly popular loans can save you thousands of dollars in interest — or double your debt. Here’s when they work.
articles.snippet: A personal loan can be a powerful tool for consolidating high-interest debt, but it’s far from a cure-all.

articles.title: Dollar Slips on Yuan Strength and US Trade Uncertainty
Yes Bank flags $0.28 million forex card fraud in Latin America
articles.snippet: Around 5,000 customers were potentially affected, but the bank’s monitoring system successfully blocked 688 unauthorized attempts, safeguarding roughly USD 0.1 million

articles.title: Rupee settles 1 paisa lower at 90.96 against US dollar
articles.snippet: Rupee settles at 90.96 against the dollar, influenced by crude oil prices and foreign fund flows.

articles.title: BookMyForex denies data leak claims, says customer funds are safe
articles.snippet: The clarification comes after a report on Wednesday flagged allegedly unauthorised transactions in US dollars and Brazilian reals, suggesting that hackers may have gained access to sensitive card details and executed international transactions without requiri…

articles.title: Solana (SOL) Recovery Shows Strength After Breaking Initial Resistance Level
articles.snippet: Solana failed to settle above $85 and trimmed some gains. SOL price is now recovering losses from $76 and showing a few positive signs. SOL price started a decent recovery wave above $78 and $80 against the US Dollar. The price is now trading above $80 and th…

articles.title: Rupee rises 6 paise to 90.89 against US dollar in early trade
articles.snippet: The rupee gained 6 paise to 90.89 against the US dollar in early trade on Wednesday aided by a weaker greenback and a strong opening in the domestic equity markets.
